---
title: "Group Member Evaluation Form"
subtitle: "IBM 6540 — Applied Machine Learning for Marketing"
author: "Jake Evans"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    embed-resources: true
---

## Evaluation Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Name | Jake Evans |
| Date | 07/22/2026 |
| Group Name / Project Name | Seis Leches (Group 1) — IBM 6540 Retail Discount & Sales Analysis |

## Instructions

Please evaluate your group members (including you) from 1 to 100% scale. If you believe that a member equally contributed to the group project output submitted, give them 100%. If you believe that a member did not do his or her fair share, assign a percentage less than 100%. Evaluation will be confidential. If a person receives grades that are less than 100% from more than one person, the average of peer evaluation will be weighted to group grade to determine individual grade. Each student must evaluate his/her peers in the absence of the other members to guarantee confidentiality and accuracy.

If there was a group leader who contributed to the group's success greatly above and beyond regular group members, that person may be identified as MVP below and may receive up to 10% of the group's score on top of the group score, if there is a consensus.

## Ratings

| # | Member Name | Grade (%) | MVP (yes/no) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (self) | Jake Evans | 100% | no |
| 2 | Mickyas Shawel | 100% | **yes** |
| 3 | Aldo Naranjo | 100% | no |
| 4 | Luis Berumen | 100% | no |
| 5 | Tim Arana | 100% | no |

## Comments on Teammate Contributions

**Mickyas Shawel** set up and maintained our GitHub repository for the semester, which everyone built on top of, and he led **RO5 (Marketing Interaction)**. His early setup work made it easy for the rest of the group to jump in and contribute consistently — **MVP for the group**.

**Aldo Naranjo** led **RO2 (Discounts & Volume)** and delivered his analysis and slides on schedule.

**Luis Berumen** led **RO3 (Discounts & Revenue)** and helped clean and verify the shared dataset.

**Tim Arana** led **RO4 (Category Differences)** and contributed solid analysis and edits throughout.

**Jake Evans (self):** I sourced the group's dataset and led **RO1 (Seasonality & Timing)**, building the working charts and analysis for that objective.

Everyone met their responsibilities this semester and the group worked well together overall.

## Appendix {.appendix}

- **GitHub Repo:** [jakevns/RStudio](https://github.com/jakevns/RStudio/tree/main/IBM6540-PeerEval)
- **GitHub Pages:** [Evans, Jake — IBM 6540 Group Evaluation](https://jakevns.github.io/RStudio/IBM6540-PeerEval/Evans%2CJake-IBM6540-GroupEval.html)
